# Privacy Is a Membrane, Not a Wall

Subtitle: `Energy finds its way`

## Description / Excerpt

A consentocratic essay on why privacy cannot be governed as perfect containment, why living systems leak, and why AI systems require consentful membranes rather than extraction-oriented walls.

Excerpt:

After hundreds of thousands of years of evolution, people still leak private data constantly. We confess things we meant to hide. We reveal ourselves through posture, timing, tone, absence, repetition, facial expression, purchases, friendships, search history, hesitation, and del
